LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JULIE YEE AND DAVID YEE'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITH ONE FINAL OPPORTUNITY TO AMEND WITHIN TWENTY DAYS. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED FACTS ESTABLISHING A BASIS FOR A CONTINUING NUISANCE. PLAINTIFF'S FACTS ALL RELATE TO THE CONSTRUCTION OF THE PATIO IN 1993 (PARAGRAPHS 7 AND 8). PARAGRAPH 14 IS A LEGAL CONCLUSION, NOT A STATEMENT OF FACT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-03-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS JULIE YEE AND DAVID YEE'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: SPENCER SMITH, ESQUIRE (415.520.6950) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. DANIEL C. TAYLOR, ESQUIRE (GILBERT KELLY CROWLEY & JENNETT, 415.352.6407) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIES THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED (TO ADDRESS WHAT PLAINTIFF IS CONTENDING NEEDS TO BE DONE TO ADDRESS THE NUISANCE), WITH THIRTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiff has alleged a continuing nuisance within the meaning of Baker v. Burbank-Glendale-Pasadena Airport (1985) 39 Cal.3d 862, 868-872. Baker directs the trial courts not to expand the concept of a permanent nuisance. Here, the closest analogous decision is Tracy v. Ferrera (1956) 144 Cal.App.2d 156, 158 cited with approval in Baker. Moreover, the Court in Baker made clear that if there is any ambiguity in the nature of the nuisance, plaintiff may elect. It is true that the 1993 construction on defendant's property is the source of the nuisance; however, the nuisance results from the periodic flow of water and plaintiff has alleged that the nuisance can be cured. This also is a factor in determining whether a nuisance is permanent or continuing. Phillips v. City of Pasadena (1945) 27 Cal.2d 104, 107-108. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
